revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
[[ja:赤城神社 (前橋市富士見町赤城山)]] {{Infobox religious building |名称 = 赤城神社 |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |image = [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) shoumen.JPG|260px]]
Haiden |location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Fujimi-machi Akagiyama|ja|富士見町赤城山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi-machi Akagiyama|1=Fujimi-machi Akagisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22125907}}4-2 |位置 = {{Coord|36|33|10.1|N|139|11|0.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=赤城神社|qq=}} |deity = Akagi Daimyōjin
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Iwatutsuo-no-Kami|ja|磐筒男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatutsuo-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}}
{{ill|Iwatutsume-no-Kami|ja|磐筒女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatutsume-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} |shintai = {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|qid=Q1595289|lt=sacred mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}) |type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) {{ill|disputed shrine|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/disputed shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
{{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} disputed
Former {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534 |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = Daidō Akagi Shrine |札所等 = |festival = {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|qid=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}} |神事 = Uzuki Hachinichi Festival
Lake Festival
Yabusame |地図={{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=赤城神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|33|10.10|N|139|11|0.56|E|qq=}}|qq=}} |Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name = '''Akagi Shrine''' (赤城神社) | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} | image = [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) shoumen.JPG|260px]]
Haiden | location = {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Fujimi, Akagi-yama|ja|富士見町赤城山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi, Akagi-yama|1=Fujimi-machi Akagisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22125907}}4-2 | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|10.1|N|139|11|0.5|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=赤城神社|qq=}} | deity = {{ill|Akagi Daimyōjin|ja|赤城大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}}
{{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}
{{ill|Iwatotsu Otokami|ja|磐筒男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatotsu Otokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}}
{{ill|Iwatotsu Onnakami|ja|磐筒女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatotsu Onnakami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}}
{{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|lt=Keitsushin|1=Futsunushi}}
{{ill|Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} | Shintai = {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} ({{ill|Sacred mountains|en|Sacred mountains|qid=Q1595289|lt=Sacred Mountain|1=Sacred mountains}}) | type = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大|lt=Meishin Taisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9610964}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
{{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}} {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}
Former {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534 | Founding = Unknown | Main shrine = | Alternate name = Daidō Akagi Shrine | Satsujo, etc = | festival = {{ill|May 8|en|May 8|qid=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}} | rites = Uzuki Eight Day Festival
Lake Water Festival
Yabusame | map = {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=赤城神社|caption=|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=10|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|33|10.10|N|139|11|0.56|E|qq=}}|qq=}} }} [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) enkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Distant view
The shrine is located on the shore of {{ill|Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|ja|大沼 (赤城山)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|1=Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437111}} at the summit of Mount Akagi.]] [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii gate]] '''Akagi Shrine''' (赤城神社) is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, specifically in the {{ill|Fujimi-machi Akagiyama|ja|富士見町赤城山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi-machi Akagiyama|1=Fujimi-machi Akagisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22125907}} area. It is considered a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and a disputed {{ill|Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja|上野国二宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Shrine of Kōzuke Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Its former Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was that of a {{ill|village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.{{Sfn|勢多郡誌編纂委員会|1958|p=1230|qq=}}Gunma Prefecture, "Jōmō Taikan," Kankodō, 1934, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1224435/1/195 p.278]. This shrine is a center of Mount Akagi worship, enshrining Akagi Daimyōjin, {{ill|Prince Toyokiirihiko|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, and several other deities. The sacred bodies are Mount Akagi and the lakes Ōnuma and Konuma at its summit. While the official name is '''Akagi Shrine''', it is also called '''Daidō Akagi Shrine''' (大洞赤城神社) to distinguish it from other shrines of the same name. It is considered one of the main shrines among the approximately 300 Akagi Shrines found throughout Japan, especially concentrated in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}. [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) enkei.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Distant view
The shrine grounds are situated at the edge of the {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} summit, by the {{ill|Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|ja|大沼 (赤城山)|lt=Ōnuma|lt_ja=大沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|1=Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437111}}.]] [[File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Akagi Shrine''' (Akagi Jinja) is a shrine located in {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Fujimi, Akagi-yama|ja|富士見町赤城山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujimi, Akagi-yama|1=Fujimi-machi Akagisan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22125907}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}) {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}, and a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}}{{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}. Its former shrine Ranking under the Modern Shrine Ranking System was {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{Sfn|勢多郡誌編纂委員会|1958|p=1230|qq=}}Gunma Prefecture, “Jōmō Daikan”, Kankodo, 1934, [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1224435/1/195 p.278]. It is a shrine of Mount Akagi faith, enshrining {{ill|Akagi Daimyōjin|ja|赤城大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}}, {{ill|Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}}, among several other deities, and it considers Mount Akagi and the lakes on its summit—the large lake (Ōno) and the small lake (Kono)—as its sacred object. Although its official name is '''Akagi Shrine''', to distinguish it from other {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}}, it is also called '''Daidō Akagi Shrine''' (Daidō–). It is one of what is presumed to be the main shrines among the approximately 300 Akagi Shrines nationwide, especially in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kanto Region|1=Kantō region}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}|qq=}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Akagi Daimyōjin|ja|赤城大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}}The enshrined deities are recorded in the shrine’s official history (the on-site explanatory board, however, lists 12 different deities). * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|Iwatotsu Otokami|ja|磐筒男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatotsu Otokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}} * {{ill|Iwatotsu Onnakami|ja|磐筒女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatotsu Onnakami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwa-tsutsu-no'o|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9010200}} * {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|lt=Keitsushin|1=Futsunushi}} * {{ill|Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} ; Side Hall * {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu-kō|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} * {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Ōyama Sakami|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}} ; Offering Hall * {{ill|Ōgetsuhime|ja|オオゲツヒメ|lt=Ōgetsuhime-no-Kami|lt_ja=大宣都比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgetsuhime|1=Ōgetsuhime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3261482}} * {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|qid=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi|1=Ukemochi}} * {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} ; Sake Hall * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} * {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} ; Kono Shrine (Toyouke Shrine) * {{ill|Kuranaitama-no-Kami|ja|倉稲魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=倉稲魂神|4=de|5=Ukanomitama|6=eo|7=Ukanomitama|8=mni|9=ꯎꯀꯅꯣꯃꯤꯇꯃ|10=ru|11=Ука-но_митама|12=simple|13=Ukanomitama|14=sv|15=Ukanomitama|16=zh|17=宇迦之御魂神|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuranaitama-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} == History == [[File:MountAkagi.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|qid=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi|1=Mount Akagi}} (object of worship)]] [[File:akagiyama_ono.jpg|thumb|right|Mount Akagi Summit
Our shrine is located on the central peninsula ({{ill|Kotori-ga-shima|ja|小鳥ヶ島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotori-ga-shima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). The area with the building at the far left of the lake is Daidō, and the mountain at the far right is {{ill|Jizōdake|ja|神庫山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jizōdake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Jizōdake).]] == Founding == The founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, it is said that {{ill|Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|2=ja|3=豊城入彦命|qid=Q11633375|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoshirihitohiko-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Toyokiirihiko|qq=}} enshrined the spirits of the mountain and marsh when he came to rule over {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Kamino|1=Kōzuke Province}}, and later, during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|qid=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}} and {{ill|Emperor Yōmei|en|Emperor Yōmei|qid=Q349281|lt=Emperor Yōmei|1=Emperor Yōmei}}, the shrine buildings were established.{{Sfn|富士見村誌編纂委員会|1979|pp=631-632|qq=}} It is said that at the time of the initial enshrinement the deity was venerated on the mid-slope of {{ill|Hokurayama|ja|神庫山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hokurayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later known as Jizōdake), but in {{ill|806|en|806|qid=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}} (the first year of {{ill|Daidō|en|Daidō|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}}), it was relocated to the southern bank of {{ill|Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|ja|大沼 (赤城山)|lt=Ōnuma|lt_ja=大沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōnuma (Mount Akagi)|1=Ono|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11437111}} (Ono) at the current old Daidō shrine grounds, and on the bank of the small marsh, the {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|小沼宮|lt=Konogū|lt_ja=このぐう|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kono Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (later known as {{ill|Toyouke Shrine|ja|豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine|qq=}}) was built.Shrine tradition record. It is said that, because of the era name, the area around the shrine came to be known as “Daidō”, and hence it is also called “Daidō Akagi Shrine”. However, it is also said that “Daidō” was written as “Daidō” (大堂), and that a hall once stood on the summit.“Akagi Shrine”. It is said that since ancient times the mountain’s {{ill|Akagi Daimyōjin|ja|赤城大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}} and the marsh deity {{ill|Akanauma Ōkami|ja|赤沼大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akanauma Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were enshrined, and that mirrors once used for worship were discovered in the lake. == Heian to Sengoku Period == In {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rokukokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are several records of bestowing shrine Ranks upon “Akagi Shrine”, and in the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}} text {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} an entry appears for the “Akagi Shrine of {{ill|Kōzuke Province|en|Kōzuke Province|qid=Q1054838|lt=Ueno Province|1=Kōzuke Province}} {{ill|Seta District, Gunma|en|Seta District, Gunma|qid=Q2324302|lt=Setagun|1=Seta District, Gunma}}” as a {{ill|Meishin Taisha|ja|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meishin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}, recognized as a {{ill|Ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}}. Among the Ronsha, besides our shrine at the summit of Mount Akagi, there are also the mountainside shrine {{ill|Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|lt=Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|lt_ja=三夜沢赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine|1=Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635467}} and the foothill shrine {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}}; however, this remains uncertain. Our shrine is presumed to be the mountain shrine (Yamamiya) of Akagi Shrine. The mountain shrine developed during the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|qid=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} period when {{ill|Shugensha|ja|修験者|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shugensha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山伏|1=Yamabushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2309838}} ascended various sacred mountains for training, making these peaks centers of their faith. Moreover, the rise of the samurai government led to a decline in the power of the imperial court and provincial governors, which in turn reduced the economic strength of the village shrine (Satemiya). As a result, while the shrine listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} (the inner shrine) declined, the influence of the mountain shrine and the central shrine (Chūsha) along the pilgrimage routes grew. In the {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō Period|1=Nanboku-chō period}}, the work {{ill|Shintōshū|en|Shintōshū|qid=Q2616931|lt=Shintōshū|1=Shintōshū}} contains three legends concerning {{ill|Akagi Daimyōjin|ja|赤城大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}}, with accounts of enshrinements at both Ōnuma and Konoma (see {{ill|Akagi Shrine#Shintōshū|ja|赤城神社#神道集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine#Shintōshū|1=Akagi Shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635468}}). This is the oldest historical source pinpointing the location of Akagi Shrine. From this description, it is interpreted that at the time the text was composed the marsh was the center of worship.“Sekai Daihyakka Jiten” ({{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}), entry on Mount Akagi. == Edo Period == Daidō Akagi Shrine first appears in historical records during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}. In {{ill|1601|en|1601|qid=Q6725|lt=1601|1=1601}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}}), {{ill|Sakai Shigetada|ja|酒井重忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakai Shigetada|1=Sakai Shigetada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5369481}}—lord of {{ill|Maebashi Castle|en|Maebashi Castle|qid=Q10898917|lt=Umabashi (Maebashi) Castle|1=Maebashi Castle}}—entered service and fervently worshipped the Daidō Akagi Shrine facing the {{ill|Kimon|ja|鬼門|1=Kimon|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimon|qq=}}, a practice followed by successive feudal lords. Shigetada petitioned the shogunate for a reconstruction of the “Shōichii Akagi Daimyōjin / Akagi Shrine”, and the work was completed. The next lord, {{ill|Sakai Tadayo|en|Sakai Tadayo|qid=Q1065113|lt=Sakai Tadayoshi|1=Sakai Tadayo}}, enshrined {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu-kō|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} in the side hall, and the Sakai family designated the abbot of Juunen-ji in Maebashi. In {{ill|1641|en|1641|qid=Q6907|lt=1641|1=1641}} (the 18th year of {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|qid=Q1192913|lt=Kan'ei|1=Kan'ei}}), the shrine buildings were entirely destroyed by lightning and rebuilt by {{ill|Sakai Tadakiyo|en|Sakai Tadakiyo|qid=Q7402769|lt=Sakai Tadayoshi|1=Sakai Tadakiyo}}.{{Sfn|富士見村誌編纂委員会|1979|pp=631-632|qq=}} Originally, the combined worship of the mountain deity and {{ill|Tōshō Daigongen|ja|東照大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōshō Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 徳川家康|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q171977}} ({{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}) attracted devotion not only from the shogunate but also from many feudal lords nationwide. The “Yamakirihiki Festival” on the eighth day of Uzuki, celebrated as the “Akagi Pilgrimage”, reportedly drew a vast number of worshipers from across the {{ill|Kanto|ja|関東|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanto|1=Kantō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11655841}} region. During this period, branch shrines were established throughout the area under shogunal protection. During the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|qid=Q1195131|lt=Kansei Period|1=Kansei}}, a dispute arose between Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine and Daidō Akagi Shrine over the usage of the terms “Honsha” and “Honkū”. In {{ill|1798|en|1798|qid=Q7823|lt=1798|1=1798}} (the 10th year of Kansei), the {{ill|Yoshida Family|ja|吉田家|1=Urabe clan|2=ja|3=卜部氏|lt=Yoshida family|comment=Might be more of a redirect with potential but this is strictly potential right now|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Family|qq=}}—which had been granted the Rank of Shōichii at Sanyazawa Akagi Shrine—was asked by Daidō Akagi Shrine for a donation recorded as “Shōichii”. The Yoshida family consulted with the Sanyazawa side, who rejected the request. However, the following year, the {{ill|Minister of Divinities|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister of Divinities|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}} and {{ill|Shirakawa Family|ja|白川家|lt=Shirakawa Hakuō Family|lt_ja=白川伯王家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白川伯王家|1=Shirakawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579697}}, who were at odds with the Yoshida family, offered a document bearing the inscription “Honsha”/“Honkū” for “Ueno Province Sōsha Daidō Akagi Shrine”. This document was to be unveiled during the annual Senju Kannon exhibition held in Maebashi. The Sanyazawa side strongly protested, and in {{ill|1800|en|1800|qid=Q6875|lt=1800|1=1800}} (the 12th year of Kansei) the abbot of Daidō Akagi Shrine at Juunen-ji together with the Shirakawa Hakuō family initiated litigation—including a request to halt the unveiling. The lord of the {{ill|Kawagoe Domain|en|Kawagoe Domain|qid=Q3034465|lt=Kawagoe Domain|1=Kawagoe Domain}}, Matsudaira, intervened as mediator; the disputed document was sealed while the scheduled unveiling at Daidō proceeded as planned. Nevertheless, the controversy continued, and in {{ill|1802|en|1802|qid=Q6883|lt=1802|1=1802}} (the 2nd year of {{ill|Kyōwa|en|Kyōwa|qid=Q960763|lt=Kyōwa|1=Kyōwa}}) the Sanyazawa side appealed to the {{ill|Jisha-bugyō|en|Jisha-bugyō|qid=Q1036279|lt=Temple and Shrine Magistrate|1=Jisha-bugyō}} of the shogunate, and a national resolution was mandated. Ultimately, an accord was reached in {{ill|1816|en|1816|qid=Q6953|lt=1816|1=1816}} (the 13th year of {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}}) whereby the document was deposited in the inner shrine and the usage of the inscription was determined by mutual agreement (as recorded in “Miyagi Mura-shi”). == From the Meiji Period Onwards == Due to the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|qid=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu Kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was separated from Juunen-ji and a new priest was appointed from among locals promoting anti-Buddhist reforms. Furthermore, plans were proposed to rebuild the shrine buildings—which had deteriorated over more than three centuries under Mount Akagi’s harsh climate—but these were never realized. Between the 20th and 43rd years of the Meiji era, several shrines within Mount Akagi, including the {{ill|Toyouke Shrine at Konoma|ja|小沼の豊受神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine at Konoma|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine on Kotori-ga-shima|ja|小鳥ヶ島の厳島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine on Kotori-ga-shima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the {{ill|Takakami Shrine on Kurohi Mountain Summit|ja|黒檜山頂の高於神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakami Shrine on Kurohi Mountain Summit|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, were merged.{{Sfn|富士見村誌編纂委員会|1954|pp=472-474|qq=}} Furthermore, under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was classified as a {{ill|Murasha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. There was even an initiative to combine the three shrines—Sanyazawa, Daidō, and Ninomiya—into a {{ill|Kokuhei Chūsha|ja|国幣中社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhei Chūsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, but this never materialized due to the end of the war. In {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 45), the shrine was relocated to the current site on Kotori-ga-shima (the former site of Itsukushima Shrine), and a new shrine building was constructed that still stands today. At that time, opposition movements arose, claiming that the environment of Kotori-ga-shima was being destroyed. In {{ill|2006|en|2006|qid=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 18), a major restoration was undertaken on the occasion of the Daidō Shrine Relocation 1200th Anniversary Festival. == Shrine Ranks == * According to the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rokukokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, there are records of bestowing shrine Ranks upon “Akagi Shrine” (though theories exist that differ for shrines other than this one). ** In the 6th year of {{ill|Jōwa (Heian period)|en|Jōwa (Heian period)|qid=Q1075605|lt=Shōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} ({{ill|839|en|839|qid=Q23874|lt=839|1=839}}) in June, a lowering to Junior Fifth Rank was recorded in “Zoku Nihon Kōki” ({{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Zoku Nihon Kōki|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}). ** In the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|Jōgan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|867|en|867|qid=Q30347|lt=867|1=867}}) on June 20, a promotion from Junior Fifth Rank Upper to Senior Fifth Rank Lower was recorded in “Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku” ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). ** In the 11th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōtoku|1=Zhenguan}} ({{ill|869|en|869|qid=Q23917|lt=869|1=869}}) on December 25, Senior Fifth Rank Upper was conferred (as per “Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku”). ** In the 16th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|qid=Q3577278|lt=Jōtoku|1=Zhenguan}} ({{ill|874|en|874|qid=Q23843|lt=874|1=874}}) on March 14, a demotion to Junior Fourth Rank Lower was recorded ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}). ** In the 4th year of {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|qid=Q747451|lt=Genkei|1=Gangyō}} ({{ill|880|en|880|qid=Q23618|lt=880|1=880}}) on May 25, a promotion to Junior Fourth Rank Upper was recorded ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) – the inscription is rendered as either “Akagi Ishigami” or “Akagi Numagami”.Variations in manuscripts result in either “Akagi Ishigami” or “Akagi Numagami”. * After the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rokukokushi|1=Rikkokushi}} period: ** Around the 3rd year of {{ill|Chōgen|en|Chōgen|qid=Q1193557|lt=Chōgen|1=Chōgen}} ({{ill|1030|en|1030|qid=Q19301|lt=1030|1=1030}}), Senior First Rank was recorded in “Ueno-koku Kōtai Jitsuroku-chō”. ** Senior First Rank is also recorded in “Ueno-koku Shinmyōchō” – the inscription being “Akagi Daimyōjin”. == Shrine facilities == == Kotori-ga-shima (Current Shrine Grounds) == The shrine grounds were relocated from the old Daidō site in 1970, and the construction of the shrine building at that time is recorded on the “Akagi Shrine Construction Donation Commemorative Stele”.{{Cite web|Language=Japanese|title=III Akagi Mountain Faith and the Historical Scenic Beauty Seen in the South Foothill Settlements|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/59/rekimachi_2shou_3.pdf|website=Maebashi City|accessdate=2025-02-04|qq=}} On Kotori-ga-shima, there is an archaeological site known as the “Kotori-ga-shima Ruins” from the Nanboku-chō period. From beneath a pagoda bearing an inscription dated to the 5th year of {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|qid=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} ({{ill|1372|en|1372|qid=Q6258|lt=1372|1=1372}}), which indicates the deposition of a Lotus Sutra, fragments of a copper scripture cylinder and ten mirrors were unearthed. These mirrors, along with the pagoda and the remnants of the scripture cylinder, have been designated as cultural properties by Gunma Prefecture.[http://www.manabi.pref.gunma.jp/bunkazai/g614157.htm Kotori-ga-shima Excavated Mirror] (Gunma Prefectural Lifelong Learning Center).[http://www.gunmaibun.org/cgi-bin/iseki/database.cgi?cmd=dp&num=22845&dp= Kotori-ga-shima Ruins] (Gunma Prefecture Buried Cultural Properties Investigation Group). File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) honden.JPG|Main Hall File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) haiden.JPG|Haiden == Daidō (Former Shrine Grounds) == File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) motomiya.JPG|Within the former shrine grounds File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) motomiya shinsui.JPG|Sacred Water File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) motomiya bentengu.JPG|Benten Shrine File:Akagi-jinja (Fujimi) motomiya torii.JPG|Former Shrine Grounds Torii == Others == * Konoma Ruins :: An archaeological site at the bottom of the small marsh where deposited mirrors have been found, dating from the late Heian period to the Edo period.[http://www.gunmaibun.org/cgi-bin/iseki/database.cgi?cmd=dp&num=22846&dp= Konoma Ruins] (Gunma Prefecture Buried Cultural Properties Investigation Group). * Great Torii (Maebashi, Fujimi Town, Kogure, {{ウィキ座標|36|26|44.91|N|139|05|48.69|E|region:JP-10_type:landmark|位置|name=大鳥居|qq=}}) :: Located at the Kogure intersection at the foot of the mountain. Height: 21.3 m; Width: 28 m. :: The original first torii, predating the current structure, stands at {{ill|Kogure Shrine|ja|小暮神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kogure Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to the northeast of the intersection. This torii was installed at the mountain trail entrance in the 4th year of {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} ({{ill|1784|en|1784|qid=Q7752|lt=1784|1=1784}}). It was later relocated to Daidō due to the construction of the Akagi toll road in the Showa era, but severe weather forced its return to Kogure.According to the explanatory stone at Kogure Shrine. == Concurrent Shrines == * {{ill|Sugawara Shrine|ja|菅原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Nichirinji Town|ja|日輪寺町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nichirinji Town|1=Nichirinjimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11510489}}) * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Shimokoide Town|ja|下小出町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimokoide Town|1=Shimokoidemachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24871744}}) * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神明神社|1=Shinmei shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590310}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Aoyagi Town|ja|青柳町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Town|1=Aoyagi Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17502940}}) * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Sekine Town|ja|関根町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekine Town|1=Sekine Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24862840}}) * {{ill|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|en|Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|qid=Q713021|lt=Atago Shrine|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Kawabata Town|ja|川端町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabata Town|1=Kawabata Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18990576}}) * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima Shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Kawahara Town|ja|川原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawahara Town|1=Kawahara Town|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18990575}}) * {{ill|Aramaki Shrine|ja|荒牧神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramaki Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Aramaki Town|ja|荒牧町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramaki Town|1=Aramakimachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11618796}}) * {{ill|Tachibana Shrine|ja|橘神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana Shrine|1=Tachibana Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544339}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Taguchi Town|ja|田口町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taguchi Town|1=Taguchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11576373}}) * {{ill|Koide Shrine|ja|小出神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koide Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}{{ill|Kami-Koide Town|ja|上小出町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Koide Town|1=Kami-Koidemachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24873430}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|qid=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}})Festival details as per the shrine’s official history. * Ninen Festival ({{ill|February 17|en|February 17|qid=Q2341|lt=February 17|1=February 17}}) * Flower Offering Festival ({{ill|April 1|en|April 1|qid=Q2510|lt=April 1|1=April 1}}) * Purification Rite ({{ill|May 7|en|May 7|qid=Q2553|lt=May 7|1=May 7}}) * Mountain Opening Festival / Annual Festival ({{ill|May 8|en|May 8|qid=Q2555|lt=May 8|1=May 8}}) * Uzuki Eight Day Festival / Flower Planting Festival (Ancestral Spirits Festival) (Lunisolar April 8) * Sacred Robe Rite (Mangatsu Kamimoro Festival) ({{ill|June 1|en|June 1|qid=Q2625|lt=June 1|1=June 1}}) * Great Purification, Dojo Festival / Fire Extinguishing Festival ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|qid=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}}) * Summer Festival (First Saturday of August) * Lakeside Festival (Marsh Deity Festival / Marsh Pacification Rite) ({{ill|August 8|en|August 8|qid=Q2780|lt=August 8|1=August 8}}) * Ancestral Spirits Festival (Requiem Festival) (August) – with a welcoming festival on the 13th and a sending-off festival on the 16th * Sacred Robe Rite (Autumn Kamimoro Festival) ({{ill|October 1|en|October 1|qid=Q2913|lt=October 1|1=October 1}}) * Autumn Festival (October Sports Day) * New Rice Harvest Festival / Kamunotsuki Festival ({{ill|November 23|en|November 23|qid=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}}) * December Great Purification, Dojo Festival / Fire Extinguishing Festival ({{ill|December 1|en|December 1|qid=Q2297|lt=December 1|1=December 1}}) == Cultural Properties == {{ill|Gunma Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties|ja|群馬県指定文化財一覧|lt=Gunma Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties|1=Gunma Prefecture-designated Important Cultural Properties|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11609593}} (Tangible Cultural Properties) [[File:小鳥が島出土鏡.JPG|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Kotori-ga-shima Excavated Mirror|qq=}}{{small|displayed at {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|en|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qid=Q20982941|lt=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|1=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History}}.|qq=}}]] * Kotori-ga-shima Excavated Mirror (with pagoda and remnants of a scripture cylinder) (archaeological material) – Held by {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|en|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qid=Q20982941|lt=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|1=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History}}. Designated on September 20, 1977 (Showa 52). The pagoda bears an inscription from the 5th year of {{ill|Ōan|en|Ōan|qid=Q1146245|lt=Ōan era|1=Ōan}} ({{ill|1372|en|1372|qid=Q6258|lt=1372|1=1372}}), and during its relocation the remnants of a scripture cylinder and ten deposited mirrors were unearthed.{{Sfn|富士見村誌編纂委員会|1979|pp=631-632|qq=}}{{Cite web |title=県指定文化財一覧: 前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-06-29 |language=ja|qq=}} == Local Information == ; Location * {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}{{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|qid=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}} Fujimi Town, Akagi-yama 4-2 ; Access * From {{ill|Maebashi Station|en|Maebashi Station|qid=Q1062416|lt=Maebashi Station|1=Maebashi Station}} ({{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q499071}} {{ill|Ryōmō Line|en|Ryōmō Line|qid=Q253391|lt=Ryomo Line|1=Ryōmō Line}}), take the {{ill|Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|ja|関越交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan-Etsu Kōtsū|1=Kan'etsu Kōtsū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11656435}} bus and alight at the “Akagi Hiroba-mae” bus stop (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes by bus, then about a 5-minute walk). == References == {{reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|Language=Japanese|title=Seta-gun Chronicle (勢多郡誌)|date=1958-03-30|year=1958|publisher=Seta-gun Chronicle Compilation Committee (勢多郡誌編纂委員会)|ref=harv|author=Seta-gun Chronicle Compilation Committee (勢多郡誌編纂委員会)|qq=}} * {{Cite book|Language=Japanese|title=Fujimi Village Chronicle (富士見村誌)|date=1954-11-23|year=1954|publisher=Fujimi Village Office (富士見村役場)|ref=harv|editor=Fujimi Village Chronicle Compilation Committee (富士見村誌編纂委員会)|qq=}} * {{Cite book|Language=Japanese|title=Fujimi Village Chronicle, Continued (富士見村誌 続編)|date=1979-10-01|year=1979|publisher=Fujimi Village Office (富士見村役場)|ref=harv|editor=Fujimi Village Chronicle Compilation Committee (富士見村誌編纂委員会)|qq=}} * Imai Zen'ichirō, ''Akagi no Kami'' (煥乎堂, 1974) * “Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Gunma Prefecture” (『日本歴史地名体系 群馬県の地名』) – Entry on Akagi-yama, Seta-gun == See Also == * {{ill|Akagi Shrines|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} ** {{ill|Akagi Shrine (Maebashi Sanyazawa Town)|ja|赤城神社 (前橋市三夜沢町)|lt=Akagi Shrines|lt_ja=赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine (Maebashi Sanyazawa Town)|1=Miyosawa Akagi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635467}} (Maebashi, Sanyazawa Town) ** {{ill|Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|ja|二宮赤城神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Akagi Shrine|qq=}} (Maebashi, Ninomiya Town) == External Links == (前橋市富士見町赤城山)]] [[ja:赤城神社]] {{Commons category|Akagi-jinja (Fujimi)}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Daidō Akagi Shrine (Q11635469)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kōzuke Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !!
